purpose: Define native dyop kernel architecture before implementation. A subkernel is eligible for full-model quality runs only after its native op-tree beats the matching materialized tensor subkernel gate.

target.fail_closed_rule: Level 2 native paths must execute native dyop kernels or fail closed; no Torch CPU reroute belongs in a native dyop op-tree.

research_basis.name: Arm Compute Library
research_basis.architecture_note: Convolution is selected among GEMM, Winograd, direct, and indirect-GEMM families with CPU-specific kernels. This argues for per-shape dyop op-trees, not one generic conv loop.

research_basis.name: Arm Neon Intrinsics Reference
research_basis.architecture_note: AArch64 Neon provides the vector loads, FMLA, and reductions used inside microkernels, but intrinsics are only the innermost primitive, not the full op-tree.

research_basis.name: gemmlowp
research_basis.architecture_note: Low-precision GEMM performance comes from packing and tiled microkernels. Dyop GEMM needs the same structure with dyadic scale application fused after accumulation.

research_basis.name: Im2win: Memory Efficient Convolution On SIMD Architectures
research_basis.architecture_note: Convolution speed depends on window/tile ordering and SIMD-friendly reuse, not merely replacing scalar inner products with vector instructions.

valid_native_kernel_tree: weight layout selected for that shape and quantization level
activation tile or indirect window tile
microkernel with fixed MRxNR or spatialxOC shape
subkernel speed gate against materialized tensor baseline

subkernels.name: embedding
active_tree: partition token rows for large batches
expand packed int16 dyadic row to float output
required_tree: sort or bucket hot token rows when the caller permits reordering
prefetch signed_odd and scale rows
vectorize int16-to-float expansion
write multiple contiguous embedding rows per worker chunk

gate.reason: Current native embedding is a row expand loop and does not beat materialized F.embedding on the measured Qwen shape.

subkernels.name: linear_gemv
active_tree: Neon float x int16 row dot
required_tree: keep active row-dot path
optionally add NR-blocked GEMV only if it beats the row-dot gate
never route GEMV through a GEMM tile with higher setup overhead

gate.reason: Measured GEMV and output-projection GEMV beat materialized tensor baselines.

subkernels.name: linear_gemm
active_tree: persistent workers partition individual output elements
run independent packed-row dot per output element
required_tree: pack MR activation rows into a contiguous tile
prepack NR output-channel dyadic weights in K-major lane order
compute MRxNR accumulators in registers
reuse each activation load across NR channels and each weight load across MR rows
apply one scale and bias per output channel
write an MRxNR output tile

gate.reason: Independent output-element dots do not reuse activation or weight loads across rows/channels.

subkernels.name: conv2d_1x1
active_tree: pack a small tile of spatial rows
run one output channel at a time over the tile
required_tree: treat 1x1 conv as GEMM with M = batch * output_h * output_w
pack MR spatial rows from NCHW into an activation tile
prepack NR output-channel dyadic weights in K-major lane order
compute MRxNR microkernel
write NCHW output using fixed plane strides

gate.reason: Current 1x1 tile worker does not beat materialized tensor conv.

subkernels.name: conv2d_3x3_same
active_tree: initialize full output plane with bias
required_tree: split border from interior
pack or indirectly address MR interior windows
prepack NR output-channel dyadic weights as K=channels*9 lane-major tiles
compute spatialxOC microkernel over multiple adjacent output positions
write contiguous output spans

gate.reason: Current direct conv loops reload windows and weights too often.

subkernels.name: conv2d_3x3_stride2
active_tree: OC8 worker for eligible output-channel counts
required_tree: pack stride-2 MR windows without deinterleave-heavy loads
prepack NR output-channel dyadic weights in K-major lane order
compute MRxNR window tile
apply dyadic scales and bias
write NCHW output planes

gate.reason: OC8 stride-2 improves over OC4 but remains below materialized tensor speed.

subkernels.name: relu_add_pool
active_tree: partition contiguous elements or output cells
required_tree: keep passing native elementwise and maxpool paths
fuse add+relu in residual blocks
avoid standalone adaptive average pool overhead by fusing or using a dedicated tiny-shape kernel
